name: Pulseless Electrical Activity
identifyBy: Any organised ECG rhythm without a palpable pulse - this strip shows ONE example pattern
Organised electrical activity on ECG - but no palpable pulse
Any rhythm can be the underlying ECG pattern (sinus, idioventricular, wide complex, narrow complex)
Rate typically slow (<60 bpm) in true PEA
Narrow QRS PEA carries better prognosis than wide QRS PEA
Clinical diagnosis: ECG activity + absent pulse
management: Confirm no pulse despite ECG activity
Identify and treat reversible cause urgently (4Hs and 4Ts)
Reassess rhythm and pulse every 2 minutes
pearls: PEA is defined by the CLINICAL finding (no pulse) - NOT by any specific ECG morphology. PEA can look like sinus rhythm, bradycardia, wide complex, or anything organised.
Most survivable arrest rhythm when cause is identified and treated quickly
Tension pneumothorax: absent breath sounds + tracheal deviation - needle decompression
Tamponade: Beck's triad - hypotension, muffled heart sounds, JVD
